thread.title: UVA launches new national security institute
thread.title_full: UVA launches new national security institute

author: Maggie Glass
title: UVA launches new national security institute
text:
CHARLOTTESVILLE, Va. (WVIR) - The University of Virginia is launching a new institute centered on national security.
The National Security Data and Policy Institute opened Friday, November 22.
Friday morning, university leaders, the Director of National Intelligence, and Virginia Senator Mark Warner gathered in UVA’s rotunda to celebrate its opening.
The institute’s new director Phillip Potter said right off the bat he has priorities.
One is dealing with a massive influx of data.
“How do you make sense of what is a chaotic data environment?” Potter asked.
Another is keeping up with global competitors.
“How do you know when you are doing well in technological areas? What areas may the US be thriving in from a research sector? What areas they may be falling behind in?” Potter said.
To answer those questions, UVA’s National Security Data and Policy Institute will gather experts in data science, artificial intelligence, research and public policy.
“The ability to bring that all together at this institute at UVA could be absolutely critical,” Senator Mark Warner said.
This all comes as the U.S. faces ongoing security threats. Warner said it’s more than just the government that needs to pay attention to what we are dealing with.
“The Chinese have infiltrated all of our telecom networks. That means they can pick individuals, and listen to their calls, see their text messaging,” Warner said.
With a $20-million two-year contract, the institute is setting off to identify those threats and protect Americans.
“I’m thrilled to get going,” Potter siad.
The institute will be located in Charlottesville off 10th St. NW
